chat.title: Legal Q&A
chat.inputPlaceholder: Ask a legal or financial rights question...
chat.emptyState: Ask a legal or financial rights question in any language.
chat.disclaimer: MzansiLegal provides legal information, not legal advice.
chat.cautiousHint: The answer is grounded in the available legislation, but the legal position still depends on more facts or context.
chat.clarificationHint: The system found a likely legal area, but it needs one more detail before giving a reliable legal conclusion.
chat.insufficientHint: The available legislation does not provide enough support to answer this responsibly yet.
chat.urgentHint: If you are in immediate danger or facing an immediate eviction, arrest, lockout, hearing, or deadline, seek official or legal help right away.
chat.guidanceNoticeTitle: Law and guidance are both in play
chat.guidanceNoticeWithLaw: Binding law remains the controlling source. Any official guidance here is included only to explain procedure or practical next steps.
chat.guidanceNoticeGuidanceOnly: This response includes official guidance for practical context. Treat it as helpful support, not the controlling legal rule.
chat.guestBannerTitle: Ask a legal or financial rights question
chat.guestBannerDesc: Ask a legal or financial rights question in any language.

rights.title: My Rights Explorer
rights.headerDesc: Explore your legal and financial rights by topic. Tap any card to learn more.
rights.knowledgeScore: Your knowledge score — you've explored {explored} of {total} rights topics
rights.academyTitle: Learn the laws every South African should know
rights.academyIntro: Open the core Acts behind everyday rights questions and work through the most important sections in each area.
rights.academyTrackHint: Each track groups important Acts and key sections for one topic so the academy teaches the law itself, not only past Q&A.
rights.academyCurrentTrackHint: Open any law below to read the key sections, grounded excerpts, and a plain-language starting point.
rights.academyTrackNotFoundTitle: This track could not be found
rights.academyTrackNotFoundBody: Return to the academy overview and choose one of the available law tracks.
rights.emptyFaqBody: Published FAQ answers will appear here once they are reviewed and made public.
rights.listenUnsupported: Audio playback is not available in this browser.
rights.shareUnavailable: Sharing is not available on this device.
rights.emptyAcademyBody: Once the core public-interest legislation is ingested here, the academy will show the most important Acts and sections to learn from.
rights.guestProgressBanner: Sign in to save your progress
rights.guestProgressDesc: Create a free account to track which rights you have explored across sessions.
rights.evictionTitle: You cannot be evicted without a court order
rights.evictionDesc: A landlord cannot remove you without a court order under the PIE Act.
rights.evictionDetail: Even if you are behind on rent, a landlord cannot simply lock you out, change the locks, cut off your water or electricity, or remove your belongings. They must apply for an eviction order through a court under the Prevention of Illegal Eviction (PIE) Act. The court will consider all circumstances, including whether you have alternative accommodation, before granting an order.
rights.evictionQuote: "No one may be evicted from their home, or have their home demolished, without an order of court made after considering all the relevant circumstances. No legislation may permit arbitrary evictions."
rights.payslipTitle: Your employer must give you a payslip
rights.payslipDesc: You have the right to written particulars of your payment.
rights.payslipDetail: Every employer must give each employee a payslip when paying remuneration. It must include the employer's details, period covered, remuneration in money, deductions, and actual amount paid.
rights.defectiveGoodsTitle: You can return defective goods within 6 months
rights.defectiveGoodsDesc: Suppliers must repair, replace, or refund unsafe or defective goods.
rights.defectiveGoodsDetail: If goods are unsafe, defective or fail to meet quality standards within 6 months of delivery, you can return them without penalty and at the supplier's risk and expense, and demand a full refund, replacement, or repair.
rights.interestRatesTitle: Maximum interest rates on loans are capped
rights.interestRatesDesc: Credit providers cannot charge interest exceeding the prescribed limits.
rights.interestRatesDetail: The National Credit Regulator sets maximum interest rates for different credit products. No credit agreement may charge more than the prescribed rate, and any excess charge is void and unenforceable.
rights.unfairDismissalTitle: You can't be fired without a hearing
rights.unfairDismissalDesc: Dismissals must be substantively and procedurally fair.
rights.unfairDismissalDetail: A dismissal is unfair if the employer fails to prove that it was for a fair reason and that a fair procedure was followed. You have the right to be told the reasons for a proposed dismissal and the right to respond.
rights.dataConsentTitle: Companies must get consent to use your data
rights.dataConsentDesc: Personal information may only be processed with your consent.
rights.dataConsentDetail: Personal information may only be processed if you consent, if it is necessary to carry out a contract, or if required by law. You can withdraw consent at any time, and the responsible party must stop processing.
